sanskrit:
कस्माच्च ते न नमेरन् महात्मन्
गरीयसे ब्रह्मणोऽप्यादिकर्त्रे ।
अनन्त देवेश जगन्निवास
त्वमक्षरं सदसतत्परं यत् ॥
transliteration: kasmāc ca te na nameran mahātman garīyase brahmaṇo ’py ādi-kartre | ananta deveśa jagan-nivāsa tvam akṣaraṁ sad-asat tat paraṁ yat ||
literal_meaning: Why should they not bow to You, O Great Soul, who are greater than Brahmā the first creator, O limitless Lord of gods, abode of the universe, imperishable, beyond both existence and non-existence?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s ego finally dissolves into awe-struck surrender; he realizes that every hierarchy he trusted collapses before the Absolute, and the only sane response is humble prostration.
psychological_state: Peak trans-personal experience: cognitive frameworks shatter, self-concept is relativized, and the psyche re-orients from agentic doing to receptive being.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving CEO suddenly grasps the cosmic scale of climate change during a silent retreat; her lifetime status symbols feel trivial and she drops to her knees in unfiltered humility.
anchor_text: Bow to the Absolute; every hierarchy bows to It.
